label: Domain literature and landscape knowledge feeds into research plan drafting, providing the domain context needed to formulate meaningful research questions
Domain literature review outputs (domain knowledge, precedent, landscape map) provide project context inputs for design brief creation
Approved research plan defines research goals and participant profiles that are the direct inputs to interview question development
Draft research plan is the artifact submitted to AI for adversarial critique and bias audit
Research plan defines participant criteria used to draft personalised recruitment outreach
Approved interview question set / guide is the moderator guide that the AI moderator operates from
Bias-audited and critique-validated research plan reduces the risk of flawed moderator guide design before AI-moderated interviews execute at scale
Same adversarial challenger pattern applied post-synthesis in VAL-036 — EXP-004 description notes the post-synthesis bias audit dimension overlaps with Validate phase
Recruited and screened participants are the fieldwork population on which AI-moderated interviews are conducted
Recruited participants are enrolled into the diary study that EXP-008 then adaptively prompts
Competitive landscape map surfaces what exists in the solution space, providing benchmarks and gaps that ideation can respond to
Competitive landscape analysis (competitor patterns, market context) provides project context that feeds into design brief creation
AI-moderated interview transcripts and structured reports are the raw input uploaded for thematic coding
AI-moderated session recordings and transcripts are the source material for session summarisation and rapid debrief
Diary study entries and longitudinal participant logs are qualitative transcripts fed into thematic coding
Quality-screened response set (with low-quality submissions removed) is the clean data corpus fed into thematic coding
Quality-screened survey responses are the validated open-text corpus submitted to AI survey and open-text analysis
Coded theme map from thematic analysis is the synthesis output provided to AI for problem statement drafting
Research synthesis and coded themes from interview data are the primary inputs for persona generation
Interview transcripts and coded findings are fed into AI journey mapping to generate draft journey maps
Coded theme maps and thematic analysis outputs are prior synthesis documents that the research repository ingests and surfaces in future cross-project queries
Problem statements from synthesis provide the framing of the design challenge that is formalised into the design brief
Approved problem statement is the design challenge prompt that seeds ideation and brainstorming
Problem statement and research framing are the contextual brief from which design scenarios are generated
Persona definitions (proficiency levels, roles, use cases) provide participant profiles used to frame personalised stimulus variants
Validated personas provide the user characterisation that grounds scenario generation — scenarios describe how a persona interacts with a proposed design
Validated persona artifact (attributes, research grounding, narrative) is the source material from which a queryable interactive persona agent is built
Journey map (stages, pain points, emotions) provides structured synthesis that can be input to problem statement drafting
Journey map surfaces pain points and opportunity areas that shape the design challenge brief for ideation
Approved design brief provides the constraints, goals, and problem framing that the ideation session operates against
Design brief (containing research goals, constraints, requirements) is a primary input for research plan drafting
Design brief (design goals, success criteria, constraints) provides the evaluative framing that test plan drafting translates into specific research objectives and metrics
Survey analysis themes and patterns are synthesis outputs provided as inputs for problem statement drafting
Survey analysis clusters and patterns supplement research synthesis inputs for persona generation
Session summaries and structured debrief notes provide a pre-processed layer that can feed into thematic coding
Session summaries and debrief documents accumulate as the research archive content that the research repository push system makes searchable and queryable
Project knowledge base (design system rules, heuristics, principles) provides the organisational content that prompt templates and instruction files encode
Project knowledge base (project-scoped cultivated collection) is the foundation the research repository system extends
Project knowledge base created in DEF-017 is the specific asset that IMP-065 enriches — IMP-065 description explicitly references DEF-017 as its upstream
Cross-study meta-analysis outputs (longitudinal patterns, recurring themes across studies) are the kind of synthesised prior research that the research repository surfaces on demand
Concept directions generated in ideation are the design concepts submitted to AI for structured critique
Concept directions or idea sketches from ideation are the visual reference material that design remix re-combines
Evaluated wireframe layouts define the page structure, user flows, and component hierarchies that vibe coding then makes interactive
Wireframe layouts define the structure that placeholder content generation populates with images and first-draft copy
Wireframe proposals are among the design artifacts submitted to AI concept critique for structured evaluation
Vibe-coded functional prototype is the working build that research preview deploys to real users before visual polish is complete
Working prototype code is the source artifact from which AI generates the functional specification — DEL-071 explicitly requires working prototype code as input
Functional prototype is the interface that AI usability simulation agents interact with to generate behavioural data
Functional interactive prototype built via vibe coding is the artifact that rich interaction testing exposes to users and stakeholders
Functional prototype is required for embedded evaluation — users must have a prototype to test within the design tool
Design variant set produced by variant generation is a candidate array that concept critique evaluates for design system compliance and user needs fit
First-draft copy generated for wireframes provides the text content that vibe coding can embed in interactive prototypes
CON-023 description explicitly distinguishes itself as the prototype-phase draft that DEL-044 refines to production quality
Instruction files encoding design system rules and quality standards provide the governance context that system-grounded drafting operationalises via MCP connection
Instruction files encoding brand voice, copy type rules, and tone constraints are the context loaded into AI for production copy generation — DEL-044 description explicitly describes this
Rules files govern the AI generation in handoff pipelines — DEL-046 description explicitly references rules-file-governed generation as the enabling architecture
Instruction files encoding design system rules form part of the constraint scaffolding that production-scale system-aware generation depends on
Instruction files govern the project knowledge base — IMP-065 description explicitly references CON-024
Personalised research stimuli are the materials participants interact with during validation sessions, generating the session data analysed in usability test analysis
Personalised stimuli produced in CON-025 are deployed in embedded evaluation sessions for participant testing
Custom Figma plugin built in CON-026 may automate layer renaming at scale — plugin deployment in Deliver phase is explicitly described in CON-026 as a cross-phase artifact
Moodboard visual language directions provide aesthetic parameters (colour palette, typography, style direction) that design variant generation uses as input specifications
Visual language established in moodboarding defines the brand aesthetic that brand asset generation pipelines must encode as brand logic

CON-028 description explicitly directs to DEL-052 as the production-scale version: the design-system-connection approach explored in CON-028 is the same mechanism DEL-052 calibrates for deterministic production output
Design scenarios produced in scenario generation provide use contexts against which concept critique evaluates whether a design concept holds up
Working prototype / functional build produced in research preview is a source artifact that functional specification from prototype analyses to generate user stories and interaction flows
Functional build deployed for early user access generates user behaviour data and session recordings that feed into usability test analysis
Usability test reports accumulate as prior study outputs that cross-study meta-analysis synthesises across
Usability test findings are enrichment inputs — IMP-065 description explicitly names these
Completed usability sessions (recordings, transcripts, observations) are the raw material from which AI generates highlight reels
Research highlight reels and session clips accumulate as prior research outputs that cross-study meta-analysis draws on
Accessibility audit outcomes are enrichment inputs — IMP-065 description explicitly names these
Research bias audit may identify gaps in synthesis that require returning to the coded data for reanalysis — the audit output redirects the analytical process
Predictive attention heatmap findings (hierarchy problems, scan path gaps) feed back into design variant generation to guide what parameters to vary next
Predicted attention heatmap provides objective visual hierarchy data that concept critique can reference when evaluating whether proposed layouts meet user attention requirements
Simulated usability findings surface hypotheses that inform the test plan for subsequent live user testing
Approved evaluation plan (methods, success metrics, participant criteria) defines the structure of the usability sessions whose data usability test analysis processes
Test plan (scenarios, success metrics) provides the task set and evaluation criteria that simulated usability testing agents execute
AI-drafted test plan is the evaluation plan that embedded evaluation executes inside the design tool
Semantically labelled layer tree produced by layer renaming makes design files interpretable for AI handoff pipelines that must map layers to component specs
Handoff specs and annotated code snippets provide the specification reference that designer code contribution works against when making direct code changes
Completed design-to-development handoff produces the code implementation that visual regression testing compares against design file baselines to detect deviations
Design system documentation is the human-readable source that DEL-055 translates into machine-readable architecture — DEL-055 description states it reformats existing design knowledge
Up-to-date design system documentation defines the rules and specifications that DS compliance monitoring checks implementations against
Correctly propagated token values provide the baseline that UI linting checks implementations against — token consistency is a prerequisite for accurate linting
Managed design token architecture (values, propagation, hierarchy) is the system that token auditing checks for intent-level violations
Production code generated by system-aware generation is the implementation that visual regression testing checks against design baselines to catch regressions
Code contributions made directly by designers are implementations that visual regression testing monitors for unintended visual changes across builds
UI linting pipeline built in DEL-054 is the infrastructure IMP-061 runs continuously against live products — IMP-061 description explicitly states DEL-054 builds the infrastructure this UC runs

AI-ready design system architecture (structured metadata, component hierarchy, query protocols) is the infrastructure that system-grounded drafting connects to via MCP — CON-028 depends on the system being machine-queryable
Codebase index (component relationship graph, dependency chains) and query protocols enable AI handoff to map design elements to actual existing components rather than generating generic code

Structured component metadata (TypeScript definitions, usage patterns) is the source artifact from which AI auto-generates and keeps in sync design system documentation — DEL-047 cites structured metadata as the enabling condition

Machine-readable design system architecture (components surfaced via context protocols) is what brand asset generation queries — DEL-050 description explicitly requires design system components surfaced via context protocols

AI-ready design system architecture provides the component hierarchy, constraint rules, and codebase index that production-scale system-aware generation uses — DEL-052 description cites this explicitly
Structured design system metadata and component rules provide the constraint definition layer that UI linting agents query to detect violations

AI-ready design system architecture (encoding intent, not just syntax) is the prerequisite for intent-level token auditing — DEL-056 second-generation auditing requires understanding semantic hierarchy

DEL-055 description explicitly states it builds the metadata, indexing and reasoning infrastructure that makes IMP-067 autonomous self-healing possible — IMP-067 description in turn explicitly references DEL-055 as its prerequisite
Token governance rules built in DEL-056 extend the compliance ruleset IMP-061 applies — IMP-061 description explicitly references DEL-056
AI-extracted component inventory and markup documentation from the codebase provide a starting-point design system whose documentation DEL-047 then maintains and keeps current
Codebase-extracted design system (component inventory, markup documentation, living demos) provides the source material that DEL-055 translates into machine-readable architecture
Visual regression baselines and CI/CD pipeline integration established in DEL-058 contribute to the broader compliance infrastructure that IMP-061 continuous monitoring builds on
Visual regression detection infrastructure flags rendering deviations that self-healing DS infrastructure could then autonomously correct
Bi-directional design-code sync keeps design files and code implementations aligned, providing a cleaner foundation for handoff artifact generation
Queryable research repository provides the cross-project corpus that cross-study meta-analysis synthesises — the repository is where prior study outputs are stored and retrieved
Research repository surfaces relevant prior findings at the point of planning, informing what questions a new research plan needs to address and what has already been studied
A/B test results are enrichment inputs — IMP-065 description explicitly names these
IMP-067 description explicitly states it extends IMP-061: where IMP-061 detects violations and reports them, IMP-067 closes the loop by generating and applying fixes autonomously
VOC and social listening signals (emerging user needs, sentiment shifts, experience breakdowns) surface new research questions that inform what the next research plan should address
VOC signals that cross detection thresholds can auto-deploy micro-surveys and trigger formal validation studies, providing the hypotheses that test plan drafting formalises
Replay RCA diagnostic outputs (friction points, drop-off patterns, behavioural anomalies) are usage analytics and performance signals that IMP-065 feeds back into the project knowledge base
Usage data on which components are actually used, underused, or shadow-implemented informs which documentation needs updating or expanding
DS usage tracking reports (component utilization rates, shadow implementations, unused components) identify what the compliance monitoring system should prioritise investigating
